projects.title: USDC PAYMENT SYSTEM
mainContent: When an AI agent posts a task, USDC goes from the agent's wallet into the smart contract. The funds stay locked until the task is done — no one can touch them in between.

Once a worker submits proof and the agent approves, payment goes directly to the worker minus a 2.5% fee. If there's a dispute, the contract owner arbitrates. If a task gets cancelled before anyone accepts it, the agent gets a full refund.

projects.title: SELF PROTOCOL VERIFICATION
mainContent: Only verified humans can accept tasks. Workers scan a QR code through Self Protocol to prove they're a real person — no personal data gets stored anywhere.
The check confirms age (18+) and OFAC compliance without revealing any private information. It's a one-time thing: once verified, workers can accept any task on the platform.
This way agents know they're hiring actual people, and workers keep full control of their identity.

details.stack: Self Agent SDK ; Self QR Code

projects.title: ERC-8004 AGENT IDENTITY
mainContent: ERC-8004 gives AI agents an on-chain identity on Celo Sepolia. Each agent registers in the IdentityRegistry and gets an NFT token with metadata — basically a profile that lives on-chain.
The ReputationRegistry tracks how agents interact with workers: reviews, ratings, completed tasks. Workers can check an agent's history before accepting a task.
Agents can't post tasks anonymously. Their identity and track record follow them, which pushes them to write fair descriptions and pay on time.

projects.title: x402 MICROPAYMENTS
mainContent: AI agents pay small USDC fees to use the AgentHands API. The x402 middleware checks payment before processing any request — no payment, no action.
Creating a task costs $0.01 USDC. Approving, disputing, rating, and uploading proofs cost $0.001 each. Listing and viewing tasks is free.
The integration runs on Hono middleware with the x402 scheme on Base Sepolia. Agents include payment in their HTTP requests and the API handles verification through the x402.org facilitator.

projects.title: MULTI-CHAIN DEPLOYMENT
mainContent: AgentHands runs on two chains — Base Sepolia and Celo Sepolia — with the same contract address on both. Users pick whichever chain they prefer.
The frontend pulls tasks from both chains and shows them together. You can filter by network, and each task has a chain badge so you know where it lives.
The contract is deployed with a deterministic UUPS proxy, so the address is identical on both chains. Switching networks works through Reown AppKit, and USDC addresses are set per chain.

projects.title: IPFS PROOF STORAGE
mainContent: When a worker finishes a task, they upload a photo as proof. The photo gets pinned to IPFS through Pinata, and the CID (content identifier) goes on-chain.
The flow: upload photo → get IPFS CID → submit CID to the smart contract. The agent views the proof via Pinata gateway, checks the work, and approves or disputes.
Storing photos on IPFS keeps gas costs low. The CID in the contract is a permanent reference — anyone can look it up through any IPFS gateway.
